reflection:
5:45 wake-up, dizzy, couldn't drop back even after half an hour of trying. You wrote it yourself: the supplements maybe masked the surface but the underlying thing is still sitting there. That's an honest read, hold onto it, that's not catastrophising, that's data.

Look at what your week actually did. Wednesday you slept 7.5 hours and ate 2300 calories and your mood was a 7. Thursday you ran 22,000 steps on 881 calories and slept 5 hours. Yesterday you walked 12k, played a padel battle with Dan where you felt your vision was off and you weren't yourself, and you ate 1484 calories. That is a real deficit. The body is not getting fed for what you're asking it to do, and your sleep debt is now 1.6 hours. The 5:45 wake is not mysterious, it's the system pulling cortisol because there's no fuel.

Pelvic floor was your worst at 5/10. You named it yourself, you sat more so it hurt more. Fair.

You've got Isola today. Before you leave, eat a real breakfast with protein and fat, not just toast. Pack a second meal for the drive. The food is the lever right now, not another supplement.

Well done logging the chocolate worry instead of hiding it. That's the work.
opener: the supplements masked it, but you weren't wrong about the underlying thing
focus_for_today: real protein+fat breakfast before Isola, pack a second meal for the drive
